X-ray crystallography was first developed in the 1910s at the University of Gottingen in Germany by Max von Laue, William Henry Bragg and William Lawrence Bragg (the father and son of the famous physicist Lord Rayleigh). They discovered that when X-rays interact with a crystal, they produce an interference pattern of alternating bright and dark bands.
Today, X-ray crystallography is not only used to determine the structure of biological molecules, but it is also used to determine the structure of non-biological molecules such as metals, salts and crystals.

    """
    frontier = [start]
    came_from = {start: None}
    cost_so_far = {start: 0}
    while frontier:
        node = frontier.pop(0)
        if node == end:
            break
        for next in graph.neighbors(node):
            new_cost = cost_so_far[node] + graph.cost(node, next)
            if next not in cost_so_far or new_cost < cost_so_far[next]:
                cost_so_far[next] = new_cost
                frontier.append(next)
                came_from[next] = node
    path = []
    current = end
    while current is not None:
        path.append(current)
        current = came_from[current]
    path.reverse()
    return path
